---
title: "Analiza snu i stylu życia – projekt regresyjny"
author: "Twoje imię"
format: html
execute:
  echo: true
  warning: false
  message: false
---

# Wprowadzenie

Celem tego projektu jest zbudowanie modeli regresyjnych przewidujących **czas snu (`Sleep Duration`)** na podstawie danych o zdrowiu i stylu życia.  
Dane pochodzą z pliku `Sleep_health_and_lifestyle_dataset.csv`.

---

# Wczytanie danych i eksploracja

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(tidymodels)
library(skimr)
library(naniar)
library(GGally)

# Wczytanie
df <- read_csv("Sleep_health_and_lifestyle_dataset.csv")

# Podgląd danych
glimpse(df)
skim(df)

# Rozkład zmiennej docelowej
df %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = `Sleep Duration`)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 30, fill = "steelblue", color = "white")

# Korelacje między zmiennymi numerycznymi
df %>%
  select(where(is.numeric)) %>%
  ggpairs()

# Braki danych
vis_miss(df)

df <- df %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.character), as.factor))

set.seed(123)
data_split <- initial_split(df, prop = 0.8, strata = `Sleep Duration`)
train_data <- training(data_split)
test_data  <- testing(data_split)

sleep_rec <- recipe(Sleep.Duration ~ ., data = train_data) %>%
  step_impute_median(all_numeric_predictors()) %>%
  step_dummy(all_nominal_predictors()) %>%
  step_normalize(all_numeric_predictors())

sleep_rec

lm_spec <- linear_reg() %>%
  set_engine("lm")

ridge_spec <- linear_reg(penalty = 0.1, mixture = 0) %>%
  set_engine("glmnet")

lasso_spec <- linear_reg(penalty = 0.1, mixture = 1) %>%
  set_engine("glmnet")

rf_spec <- rand_forest(trees = 500) %>%
  set_engine("randomForest") %>%
  set_mode("regression")

xgb_spec <- boost_tree(trees = 500, learn_rate = 0.05) %>%
  set_engine("xgboost") %>%
  set_mode("regression")

set.seed(123)
folds <- vfold_cv(train_data, v = 5)

lm_wf <- workflow() %>% add_model(lm_spec) %>% add_recipe(sleep_rec)
rf_wf <- workflow() %>% add_model(rf_spec) %>% add_recipe(sleep_rec)
xgb_wf <- workflow() %>% add_model(xgb_spec) %>% add_recipe(sleep_rec)

lm_res <- fit_resamples(lm_wf, folds, metrics = metric_set(rmse, mae, rsq))
rf_res <- fit_resamples(rf_wf, folds, metrics = metric_set(rmse, mae, rsq))
xgb_res <- fit_resamples(xgb_wf, folds, metrics = metric_set(rmse, mae, rsq))

collect_metrics(lm_res)
collect_metrics(rf_res)
collect_metrics(xgb_res)

# Zbiorcze porównanie wyników
res <- bind_rows(
  collect_metrics(lm_res) %>% mutate(model = "Linear"),
  collect_metrics(rf_res) %>% mutate(model = "RandomForest"),
  collect_metrics(xgb_res) %>% mutate(model = "XGBoost")
)

res %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = model, y = mean, fill = .metric)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge") +
  labs(title = "Porównanie modeli", y = "Średnia metryka (CV)")

# Trenowanie najlepszego modelu (przykładowo: RandomForest) na pełnym zbiorze treningowym
best_wf <- rf_wf %>% fit(data = train_data)

# Ewaluacja na zbiorze testowym
predict(best_wf, test_data) %>%
  bind_cols(test_data %>% select(Sleep.Duration)) %>%
  metrics(truth = Sleep.Duration, estimate = .pred)
```
